meaning: adj.未听到的,未被倾听的,未知的
examples: Riots are almost unheard of in Japan.
It's almost unheard of for a child to show up hungry to school.
Pay cuts, unheard of during times of growth, may even be imposed.
This year's broadcast begins with a moment from 2008's A Jazz Piano Christmas, unheard on the radio broadcast.
Efficiency for power supply to the motherboard exceeds 94% which is unheard of. 22% fewer materials.
